created_for: GS LinkOps AI resource database building

records.name: Azure Orbital Ground Station retirement and infrastructure disposition signal
records.structured_summary: Current public reporting indicates that Microsoft retired the Azure Orbital ground-station service after an earlier commercial rollout and subsequently disposed of associated antenna assets. For GS LinkOps AI, this is a high-value provider-lifecycle case: a technically integrated cloud ground-station service can exit the market, creating migration, contract, endpoint, data-residency and mission-continuity risks even when the underlying cloud platform remains available.
records.source_type: current secondary summary referencing industry reporting
records.confidence_level: Medium. The service-retirement signal is valuable, but effective dates, asset buyer, customer migration arrangements and official Microsoft notices require primary-source confirmation.
pending_confirmation_fields: affected Azure regions and ground sites
status of historical APIs and SDKs
records.next_action: Create provider lifecycle events as append-only records rather than overwriting historical provider capabilities. Locate official Microsoft lifecycle documentation and asset-transfer records before using this event operationally.

records.name: SSC CONNECT owned-station and partner-station network model
records.structured_summary: Public network descriptions of SSC Space show a mixed infrastructure model combining company-owned ground stations with contracted or partner stations across multiple countries. This is important for GS LinkOps AI because a provider-level network entry does not prove uniform ownership, engineering configuration, security controls, licensing responsibility, SLA or booking authority at every listed site.
records.confidence_level: Medium. The mixed owned/partner network model is credible, but per-site ownership, current availability and technical capability must be validated against SSC capability sheets or contracts.
pending_confirmation_fields: owned versus partner classification per site
records.next_action: Add a required asset_provenance layer between provider and station records. Do not inherit provider-wide capability claims automatically to partner sites.

records.name: Scalable hierarchical ground-station selection for large LEO constellations
records.structured_summary: A 2025 research paper proposes a hierarchical ground-station selection framework for large LEO constellations. It decomposes the global optimization problem into single-satellite, short-time-window subproblems, clusters consistently valuable locations, and maps those clusters back to feasible GSaaS sites. The method remains computationally usable where exact mixed-integer optimization becomes difficult for larger constellations.
records.confidence_level: High for the published optimization method and reported experiments; Medium for direct commercial use without current provider, weather, licensing, pricing and capacity data.
records.next_action: Store optimization outputs with scenario, input snapshot, objective weights and confidence. Never write a recommended station portfolio without preserving the provider-data version used to generate it.
